rulings.Case Title: DAVID W. GUERDAT VS. SALLY A. UNTERBERGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff David Guerdat'S Motion To Compel Discovery And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 19, 2013, Line 3, Plaintiff David Guerdat'S Motion To Compel Discovery And Request For Sanctions Plaintiff's motion is granted. Defendant Sally Unterberger is ordered to appear to complete her deposition, if she has not already done so, on a week selected by Plaintiff between the date of this order and October 4, 2013. Defendant's counsel are ordered to pay sanctions of $1000.00 pursuant to CCP §§2023.030(a), 2023.010(d) and 2025.450(g)(1). Defendant's counsel did not act with substantial justification in delaying the rescheduling of the Defendant's deposition, after it was suspended two hours in to the deposition--at the request of Defendant--on account of her feeling too ill to continue. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: MINDY FREILE VS. TIM LINCECUM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Terminating Sanctions, Or, In The Alternative, Issue Sanctions Based On Pltf'S Failure To Comply With This Court'S Jul 26, 2013 Discovery Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 19, 2013, Line 2: Defendant TIM LINCECUM'S Motion for Terminating Sanctions, Or, In The Alternative, Issue Sanctions Based On Plaintiff's Failure To Comply With This Court's July 26, 2013 Discovery Order Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. Hearing required. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ERNICE FAGAN VS. SAN FRANCISCO GENERAL HOSPITAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion O Vacate Dismissal Of Action
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 19, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFF ERNICE FAGAN'S Motion TO Vacate Dismissal Of Action Plaintiff's motion to set aside dismissal is denied as untimely. Plaintiff states that she voluntarily dismissed this case (516891) ("Case 1") on June 26, 2012, "in anticipation of the hearing that was set for the demurrer" on her Second Amended Cross Complaint that was then scheduled to be heard on June 27, 2012. (Memo. at 1.) Plaintiff then filed another case (525904) ("Case 2") on November 7, 2012 regarding the same incident that is the subject of Case 1. Plaintiff did not file the present motion to set aside her voluntary dismissal in Case 1 until June 20, 2013. This is not within six months of the dismissal that was entered on June 26, 2012, and which is required by statute. (CCP § 473(b).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: PROFESSIONAL COLLECTION CONSULTANTS (PCC) VS. ROBERT M. LUJAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Stay Action Pending Appeal Of Ruling On Demurrer
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 19, 2013, Line 5, PLAINTIFF PROFESSIONAL COLLECTION CONSULTANTS (PCC) Mtn To Stay Action Pending Appeal Of Ruling On Demurrer The motion for stay the entire action is granted. The Court's order of August 2, 2013, is appealable per CC 1714.10(d) and Evans v. Pillsbury, Madison & Sutro (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 599, 602-604. The automatic stay provision of CCP 916 applies. While CC 1714.10(d) states that the procedure is a "special proceeding of a civil nature," it also states that an order resolving the matter "shall be appealable as a final judgment in a civil action." Id. This last portion of subsection (d) suggests that the appeal is tantamount to an appeal in a civil action and thus that CCP §916 applies. A stay of the entire action is also reasonable to insure that proceedings in this Court do not create a conflict of interest between attorney Garen and his client defendant PCC, which may affect the appeal and render the appeal ineffective. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: OLIVER J. MAR, TRUSTEE OF THE MAR LIVING TRUST VS. TON KIANG RESTAURANT, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 19, 2013, Line 11, CROSS DEFENDANT OLIVER J. MAR, TRUSTEE OF THE MAR LIVING TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT The demurrer to the breach of contract cause of action is overruled. The parties' settlement agreement does not expressly allege that defendant is responsible for curing the 3/29/05 notice of violation and defendant's interpretation of the settlement agreement is not facially unreasonable. See Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc., (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239 ("While plaintiff's interpretation of the contract ultimately may prove invalid, it was improper to resolve the issue against her solely on her own pleading. 'In ruling on a demurrer, the likelihood that the pleader will be able to prove his allegations is not the question.'")(citation omitted). The demurrer to the declaratory relief cause of action is overruled. Defendant pleads an actual controversy. In addition, a general demurrer is usually not an appropriate method for testing the merits of a declaratory relief action because the cross-complainant is entitled to a declaration of rights even if it is adverse to the cross complainant's interest. Qualified Patients Ass'n v. City of Anaheim (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 734, 751. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 19, 2013, Line 5: Plaintiff JUDITH FOY'S Motion for Order Compelling Responses to Form Interrogatories, Set One, and Requests for Production of Documents, Sets One and Two, and for Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Complt
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 19, 2013, Line 14, PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA RESTAURANT MUTUAL BENEFIT CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Complaint The motion is continued to September 27, 2013, to give the moving party a chance to comply with SF Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party shall provide a courtesy copy of its papers by September 20, 2013, with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: TRECIA KNAPP VS. GEICO GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 19, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANT GEICO GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendant's demurrer is overruled as to the entire complaint and as to the first (and only) cause of action for breach of contract. Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts to state a cause of action for breach of contract, including the essential terms of the agreement, the alleged breach on or about December 6, 2012, and damages. The Complaint is not uncertain. The Court has jurisdiction to hear this matter. The terms of the written agreement, as alleged on the face of the Complaint or matters that are subject to judicial notice, do not state that Plaintiff is required to demand arbitration. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibit C, and otherwise overruled. Defendant's procedural remedy, if it chooses, is to move to compel arbitration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: ENID SHOOR et al VS. MICHAEL BOZORA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 19, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANT RUZZO SCHOLL & MURPHY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Ruzzo Scholl & Murphy's Demurrer to Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is overruled. Plaintiffs have plead facts sufficient to establish an agency relationship between Defendant Mark Williams and Defendant Ruzzo Scholl & Murphy. (See, e.g., FAC 10, 14, 61, 86, 89-91) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ENID SHOOR et al VS. MICHAEL BOZORA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 19, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANT SARATOGA FAMILY COUNSELING, INC INCORRECTLY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Mark Williams, Inc.'s Demurrer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is sustained with leave to amend. The second through eleventh causes of action fail to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action against this defendant. Plaintiffs have not plead facts sufficient to establish the involvement, if any, Defendant has in the matter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL G. SCHINNER VS. RAYMOND ZHANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Cjh & Associates And Chijeh Hu'S Demurrer To Complaint (Taken Off Calendar On 8/08/13 In Error)
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, September 19, 2013, Line 19, Defendants Cjh & Associates And Chijeh Hu'S Demurrer To Complaint (Taken Off Calendar On 8/08/13 In Error) Overruled. Plaintiff alleges a right to recover from Zhangs (Complaint, 20), the filing of a Notice of Attorneys Lien on moving Defendants CJH/Hu ( 29), and that CJH/Hu had notice of the attorneys' lien prior to disbursing settlement funds ( 30). Plaintiff does not allege that Defendants CJH/Hu were the agents of Zhang or Zhangs LLC. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled causes of action for intentional interference with contract and conversion. See Plummer v. Day/Eisenberg, LLP 184 Cal. App. 4th 38 (2010). Defendants raise factual issues that cannot be resolved on demurrer regarding the Settlement Agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm
